hints.ablative: While 'Catilina' could be ablative (1st decl. masc.), here it is the subject
hints.vocative: Sallust is describing Catiline, not addressing him
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'Catilinae' — 'Catilina' is nominative here

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'Catilinae' with -ae is 1st declension (masculine)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Catilinae' with -ae is 1st declension
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'Catilinae' with -ae is 1st declension

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'nobilis' — 'nobili' with -i is dative/ablative singular
hints.dative: While 'nobili' could be dative, here it modifies 'genere' in the ablative of source
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'nobilis' — 'nobili' is ablative here

steps.reveal: genere = from a family/birth (abl. of source)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'genus' — 'genere' with -e is ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'genus' — 'genere' is ablative of source with 'natus'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'generis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'generis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'generis' ends in -is

hints.present active participle: Present active participle would be 'nascens' — 'natus' is perfect participle
hints.supine: Supine would be 'natum' — 'natus' is nominative, agreeing with 'Catilina'
hints.gerundive: Gerundive would be 'nascendus' — 'natus' is perfect participle

steps.prompt: The infinitive of 'natus fuit' translates to?

hints.to swim: That would be 'natare' — 'natus' comes from 'nasci' (to be born), a deponent
hints.to know: That would be 'noscere' — 'natus' comes from 'nasci' (to be born)
hints.to obtain: That would be 'nancisci' — 'natus' comes from 'nasci' (to be born)

hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'natus fuerat' or 'natus erat' — 'natus fuit' is perfect
hints.present indicative: Present would be 'nascitur' — 'natus fuit' is perfect indicative
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'natus fuerit' — 'natus fuit' is indicative

hints.nato, natare, natavi, natatum: These are the parts of 'natare' (to swim)
hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are the parts of 'noscere' (to learn/know)
hints.nanciscor, nancisci, nactus sum: These are the parts of 'nancisci' (to obtain)

translation: Catiline was born of a noble family
